Q 1[2020]

Prove that {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} is an irrational number.

Solution

To prove whether {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}  is rational or irrational, we find the square root of {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} by long division method.

{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} = 1.732050807
We observe that the decimal representation of{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} is neither terminating nor repeating. Hence, {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} is an irrational number.

Q 2[2015, 2010]

Express the decimal {tex}2 . \overline{93}{/tex} in the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}, where p, q are integers and q ≠ 0.

Solution

Let x = {tex}2 . \overline{93}{/tex} = 2.9393  ….   ------(i)
Multiply eq(i) by 100, we get
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 100x = 293.939 ….      -------(ii)
On subtracting (i) from (ii), we get
99x = 291
{tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{291}{99}=\frac{97}{33}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore 2 . \overline{93}=\frac{97}{33}{/tex}

Q 3[2015, 2011]

Simplify: {tex}\left[5\left(8^{\frac{1}{3}}+27^{\frac{1}{3}}\right)^{3}\right]^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\left[5\left(8^{\frac{1}{3}}+27^{\frac{1}{3}}\right)^{3}\right]^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}
 ={tex}\left[5\left(\left(2^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}+\left(3^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}\right)^{3}\right]^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}
= {tex}\left[5\left(\left(2\right)^{\frac{1}{3}\times 3}+\left(3\right)^{\frac{1}{3}\times3}\right)^{3}\right]^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}
={tex}\left[5(2+3)^{3}\right]^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}
={tex}\left[5(5)^{3}\right]^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}
={tex}\left[5^{4}\right]^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}
= 5

Q 4[2015, 2012]

Locate {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}  on the number line.

Solution

Let point A represents 1 as shown in Figure.
Clearly, {tex}OA = 1 \ unit.{/tex}
Now, draw a right triangle OAB in which {tex}AB = OA = 1\ unit.{/tex}
By Using Pythagoras theorem, we have
{tex}OB^2 = OA^2 + AB^2 \\= 1^2 + 1^2{/tex}
{tex}= 2 {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow O B=\sqrt{2}{/tex}

Taking O as centre and OB as a radius draw an arc intersecting the number line at point P.
Then p corresponds to {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} on the number line. Now draw DB of unit length perpendicular to OB.
By using Pythagoras theorem, we have
{tex}OD^2 = OB^2 + DB^2{/tex}
OD2 = {tex}(\sqrt{2})^{2}{/tex} + 12
{tex}= 2 + 1 = 3{/tex}
OD = {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}
Taking O as centre and OD as a radius draw an arc which intersects the number line at the point Q.
Clearly, Q corresponds to {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}.

Q 5[2015]

Visualize 3.765 on the number line using successive magnification.

Solution

We know that the number 3.765 will lie between 3.764 and 3.766.
We know that the numbers 3.764 and 3.766 will lie between 3.76 and 3.77.
We know that the numbers 3.76 and 3.77 will lie between 3.7 and 3.8.
We know that the numbers 3.7 and 3.8 will lie between 3 and 4.
Therefore, we can conclude that we need to use the successive magnification, after locating numbers 3 and 4 on the number line

Q 6[2015]

If x = 2 + {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}, find the value of x3 + {tex}\frac{1}{x^3}{/tex}.

Solution

We have,
x = 2 + {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2+\sqrt{3}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2+\sqrt{3}} \times \frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{2-\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{(2+\sqrt{3})(2-\sqrt{3})}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{(2)^{2}-(\sqrt{3})^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{4-3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2-\sqrt{3}}{1}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex} = 2 - {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}
Now, x3 + {tex}\frac{1}{x^3}{/tex} = (x + {tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex}) [x2 - x {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex} + ({tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex})2]
= (2 + {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} + 2 - {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}) [(2 + {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex})2 - 1 + (2 - {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex})2]
= 4[(2)2 + ({tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex})2 + 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} - 1 + (2)2 + (-{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex})2 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}] 
= 4[4 + 3 + 4{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} - 1 + 4 + 3 - 4{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}]
= 4[13]
= 52

Q 7[2015]

Prove that: {tex}\frac{a^{-1}}{a^{-1}+b^{-1}}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{a^{-1}}{a^{-1}-b^{-1}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 b^{2}}{b^{2}-a^{2}}{/tex}

Solution

 simplify the L.H.S, 
{tex}\frac{a^{-1}}{a^{-1}+b^{-1}}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{a^{-1}}{a^{-1}-b^{-1}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\frac 1a}{\frac{1}{a}+\frac{1}{b}}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{\frac 1a}{\frac{1}{a}-\frac{1}{b}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{a} \cdot \frac{a b}{b+a}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{a} \cdot \frac{a b}{b-a}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{b}{b+a}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{b}{b-a}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{b(b-a)+b(b+a)}{(b+a)(b-a)}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{b^{2}-b a+b^{2}+a b}{b^{2}-a^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 b^{2}}{b^{2}-a^{2}}{/tex}
Hence Proved.

Q 8[2015]

Find the value of {tex}\frac{4}{{{{(216)}^{ - \frac{2}{3}}}}} + \frac{1}{{{{(256)}^{ - \frac{3}{4}}}}} + \frac{2}{{{{(243)}^{ - \frac{1}{5}}}}}{/tex}

Solution

We have {tex}\frac{4}{{{{(216)}^{ - \frac{2}{3}}}}} + \frac{1}{{{{(256)}^{ - \frac{3}{4}}}}} + \frac{2}{{{{(243)}^{ - \frac{1}{5}}}}}{/tex}
{tex}= 4{(216)^{\frac{2}{3}}} + {(256)^{\frac{3}{4}}} + 2{(243)^{\frac{1}{5}}}{/tex}
{tex}= 4{({6^3})^{\frac{2}{3}}} + {({4^4})^{\frac{3}{4}}} + 2{({3^5})^{\frac{1}{5}}}{/tex}
{tex} = 4 \times {6^{3 \times \frac{2}{3}}} + {4^{4 \times \frac{3}{4}}} + 2 \times {3^{5 \times \frac{1}{5}}}{/tex}
{tex} = 4 \times {6^2} + {4^3} + 2 \times 3{/tex}
= 144 + 64 + 6 = 214

Q 9[2015]

Simplify: {tex}\left(1^{3}+2^{3}+3^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\left(1^{3}+2^{3}+3^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}} {/tex}
= {tex}(1+8+27)^{\frac{1}{2}} {/tex}
= {tex}(36)^{\frac{1}{2}} {/tex}
= {tex}\left(6^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}} {/tex}
= {tex}6^{2 \times \frac{1}{2}}{/tex} {tex}\left[\because\left(a^{m}\right)^{n}=a^{m n}\right]{/tex}
=6
 

Q 10[2015]

Show that: {tex}\left(x^{\frac{1}{a-b}}\right)^{\frac{1}{a-c}}\left(x^{\frac{1}{b-c}}\right)^{\frac{1}{b-a}}\left(x^{\frac{1}{c-a}}\right)^{\frac{1}{c-b}}{/tex} = 1

Solution

LHS ={tex}\left(x^{\frac{1}{a-b}}\right)^{\frac{1}{a-c}}\left(x^{\frac{1}{b-c}}\right)^{\frac{1}{b-a}}\left(x^{\frac{1}{c-a}}\right)^{\frac{1}{c-b}}{/tex} 
= {tex}x^{\frac{1}{(a-b)} \times \frac{1}{(a-c)}} \times x^{\frac{1}{b-c} \times \frac{1}{b-a}} x^{\frac{1}{(c-a)} \times \frac{1}{(c-b)}}{/tex}
= {tex}x^{\frac{1}{{(a-b)(a-c)}}+\frac{1}{(b-c)(b-a)}+\frac{1}{(c-a)(c-b)}}{/tex}   [using  am{tex}\times{/tex}an = am+n]
= {tex}x^{\frac{-1}{{(a-b)(c-a)}}+\frac{-1}{(b-c)(a-b)}+\frac{-1}{(c-a)(b-c)}}{/tex}
= {tex}x^{-\frac{-b+c-c+a-a+b}{(a-b)(b-c)(c-a)}}{/tex}
= {tex}x^{-\frac{0}{(a-b)(b-c)(c-a)}}{/tex}
= x0
= 1
= RHS

Q 11[2015]

Express in the form of {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}:{tex}0 . \overline{38}+1 . \overline{27}{/tex}

Solution

Let x ={tex}0 . \overline{38}{/tex}
i.e. x = 0.3838….  ….(i)
Multiply eq. (i) by 100 we get, 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 100x = 38.3838….  ….(ii)
On subtracting eq. (i) from (ii), we get
100x - x = 38.3838... - 0.3838...
99x = 38
{tex}\Rightarrow x=\frac{38}{99}{/tex}
Let y = {tex}1 . \overline{27}{/tex}
i.e. y = 1.2727….(iii)
Multiply eq. (i) by 100 we get, 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 100y = 127.2727…(iv)
On subtracting (iii) from (iv), we get
100y-y = 127.2727.... - 1.2727....
99y = 126
{tex}\Rightarrow y=\frac{126}{99}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore x+y=0 . \overline{38}+1 . \overline{27}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{38}{99}+\frac{126}{99}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{38+126}{99}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{164}{99}{/tex}

Q 12[2015]

Rationalise the denominator:{tex}\frac{3}{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{2}}{/tex}.

Solution

{tex}\frac{3}{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3}{(\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{5})-\sqrt{2}} \times \frac{(\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{5})+\sqrt{2}}{(\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{5})+\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3(\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{2})}{(\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{5})^{2}-\sqrt{2}^{2}}{/tex} [ a2 - b2 = (a + b)(a - b)]
= {tex}\frac{3 \sqrt{3}+3 \sqrt{5}+3 \sqrt{2}}{(3+5+2 \sqrt{15})-2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3 \sqrt{3}+3 \sqrt{5}+3 \sqrt{2}}{8+2 \sqrt{15}-2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3 \sqrt{3}+3 \sqrt{5}+3 \sqrt{2}}{6+2 \sqrt{15}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3 \sqrt{3}+3 \sqrt{5}+3 \sqrt{2}}{6+2 \sqrt{15}} \times \frac{6-2 \sqrt{15}}{6-2 \sqrt{15}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{18 \sqrt{3}-6 \sqrt{45}+18 \sqrt{5}-6 \sqrt{75}+18 \sqrt{2}-6 \sqrt{30}}{6^{2}-(2 \sqrt{15})^{2}}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} a2 - b2 = (a + b)(a - b)]
= {tex} \frac{18 \sqrt{3}-6 \sqrt{9 \times 5}+18 \sqrt{5}-6 \sqrt{25 \times 3}+18 \sqrt{2}-6 \sqrt{30}}{36-60} {/tex}
= {tex}\frac{18 \sqrt{3}-18 \sqrt{5}+18 \sqrt{5}-30 \sqrt{3}+18 \sqrt{2}-6 \sqrt{30}}{-24}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{-12 \sqrt{3}+18 \sqrt{2}-6 \sqrt{30}}{-24}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{-6(2 \sqrt{3}-3 \sqrt{2}+\sqrt{30})}{-24}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2 \sqrt{3}-3 \sqrt{2}+\sqrt{30}}{4}{/tex}

Q 13[2015]

Rationalise the denominator: {tex}\frac{4}{2+\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{7}}{/tex}.

Solution

{tex}\frac{4}{2+\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{7}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{4}{(2+\sqrt{3})+\sqrt{7}} \times \frac{(2+\sqrt{3})-\sqrt{7}}{(2+\sqrt{3})-\sqrt{7}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{4(2+\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{7})}{(2+\sqrt{3})^{2}-\sqrt{7}^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{8+4 \sqrt{3}-4 \sqrt{7}}{4+4 \sqrt{3}+3-7}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{8+4 \sqrt{3}-4 \sqrt{7}}{4 \sqrt{3}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{8+4 \sqrt{3}-4 \sqrt{7}}{4 \sqrt{3}} \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{8 \sqrt{3}+12-4 \sqrt{21}}{12}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2 \sqrt{3}+3-\sqrt{21}}{3}{/tex}

Q 14[2015]

Assuming that x, y, z are positive real number, simplify: {tex}\sqrt[4]{81 x^{8} y^{4} z^{16}} \div \sqrt[3]{27 x^{3} y^{6} z^{9}}{/tex}.

Solution

{tex}\sqrt[4]{81 x^{8} y^{4} z^{16}} \div \sqrt[3]{27 x^{3} y^{6} z^{9}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\left(81 x^{8} y^{4} z^{16}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}}}{\left(27 x^{3} y^{6} z^{9}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}} {/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(81)^{\frac{1}{4}}\left(x^{8}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}} \cdot\left(y^{4}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}} \cdot\left(z^{16}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}}}{(27)^{\frac{1}{3}} \cdot\left(x^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}} \cdot\left(y^{6}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}} \cdot\left(z^{9}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{\left(3^{4}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}} \cdot x^{\left(8 \times \frac{1}{4}\right)} \cdot y^{\left(4 \times \frac{1}{4}\right)} \cdot z^{\left(16 \times \frac{1}{4}\right)}}{\left(3^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}} \cdot x^{\left(3 \times \frac{1}{3}\right)} \cdot y^{\left(6 \times \frac{1}{3}\right)} \cdot z^{\left(9 \times \frac{1}{3}\right)}} {/tex}
= {tex}\frac{3^{\left(4 \times \frac{1}{4}\right)} \cdot x^{2} \cdot y z^{4}}{3^{\left(3 \times \frac{1}{3}\right)} \cdot x y^{2} z^{3}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{x^{(2-1)} \cdot z^{(4-3)}}{y^{(2-1)}} {/tex}
= {tex}\frac{x z}{y}{/tex}

Q 15[2015]

Simplify: (14641)0.25.

Solution

146410.25
{tex}=(14641)^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}
{tex}=\left(11^{4}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}

Q 16[2015]

Evaluate: {tex}\frac{2^{0}+7^{0}}{5^{0}}{/tex}.

Solution

{tex}\frac{2^{0}+7^{0}}{5^{0}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1+1}{1}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2}{1}{/tex}
= 2

Q 17[2015]

Prove that: {tex}\left[8^{-\frac{2}{3}} \times 2^{\frac{1}{2}} \times 25^{-\frac{5}{4}}\right] \div\left[32^{-\frac{2}{5}} \times 125^{-\frac{5}{6}}\right]=\sqrt{2}{/tex}.

Solution

LHS
{tex}=\left[8^{-\frac{2}{3}} \times 2^{\frac{1}{2}} \times 25^{-\frac{5}{4}}\right] \div\left[32^{-\frac{2}{5}} \times 125^{-\frac{5}{6}}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\left[2^{3 \times\left(-\frac{2}{3}\right)} \times \sqrt{2} \times 5^{2 \times\left(-\frac{5}{4}\right)}\right] \div\left[2^{5 \times\left(-\frac{2}{5}\right)} \times 5^{3 \times\left(-\frac{5}{6}\right)}\right] {/tex}
{tex} =\left[2^{-2} \times \sqrt{2} \times 5^{\left(-\frac{5}{2}\right)}\right] \div\left[2^{-2} \times 5^{\left(-\frac{5}{2}\right)}\right] {/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2^{-2} \times \sqrt{2} \times 5^{\left(-\frac{5}{2}\right)}}{2^{-2} \times 5^{\left(-\frac{5}{2}\right)}}{/tex}

{tex}=\sqrt{2}{/tex}
= RHS

Q 18[2015]

Prove that: {tex}\left[7\left\{(81)^{\frac{1}{4}}+(256)^{\frac{1}{4}}\right\}^{\frac{1}{4}}\right]^{4}=16807{/tex}.

Solution

LHS
{tex}=\left[7\left\{(81)^{\frac{1}{4}}+(256)^{\frac{1}{4}}\right\}^{\frac{1}{4}}\right]^{4}{/tex}
{tex}=\left[7\left\{3^{4 \times \frac{1}{4}}+4^{4 \times \frac{1}{4}}\right\}^{\frac{1}{4}}\right]^{4}{/tex}
{tex}=\left[7\{3+4\}^{\frac{1}{4}}\right]^{4}{/tex}
{tex}=\left[7\{7]^{\frac{1}{4}}\right]^{4}{/tex}
{tex}=7^{4} \times 7^{\frac{1}{4} \times 4}{/tex}
= 74 {tex}\times{/tex} 7
= 75
= 16807
= RHS

Q 19[2015]

Find the value of x: {tex}\frac{3^{3 x} \cdot 3^{2 x}}{3^{x}}=\sqrt[4]{3^{20}}{/tex}.

Solution

 {tex}\frac{3^{3 x} \cdot 3^{2 x}}{3^{x}}=\sqrt[4]{3^{20}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{3^{3 x+2 x}}{3^{x}}=3^{20 x \frac{1}{4}}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{3^{5 x}}{3^{x}}=3^{5}{/tex}
34x = 35
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x = 5
{tex}x=\frac{5}{4}{/tex}

Q 20[2015]

Write the following in ascending order of magnitude {tex}\sqrt[6]{6}, \sqrt[3]{7}, \sqrt[4]{8}{/tex}.

Solution

We can write
{tex}\sqrt[6]{6}=6^{\frac{1}{6}}{/tex}
{tex}\sqrt[3]{7}=7^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex}
{tex}\sqrt[4]{8}=8^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}
LCM of 6,3 and 4 is 12
{tex}\sqrt[6]{6}=6^{\frac{1}{6}}=6^{\frac{1}{6}} \times \frac{2}{2}=6^{\frac{2}{12}}=\left(6^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{12}}=(36)^{\frac{1}{12}}{/tex}
{tex}\sqrt[3]{7}=7^{\frac{1}{3}}=7^{\frac{1}{3}} \times \frac{4}{4}=7^{\frac{4}{12}}=\left(7^{4}\right)^{\frac{1}{12}}=(2401)^{\frac{1}{12}}{/tex}
{tex}\sqrt[4]{8}=8^{\frac{1}{4}}=8^{\frac{1}{4}} \times \frac{3}{3}=8^{\frac{3}{12}}=\left(8^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{12}}=(512)^{\frac{1}{12}}{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} 36<512<2401
{tex}(36)^{\frac{1}{12}}<(512)^{\frac{1}{12}}<(2401)^{\frac{1}{12}}{/tex}
Therefore, {tex}\sqrt[6]{6}<\sqrt[4]{8}<\sqrt[3]{7}{/tex}

Q 21[2015]

Find the value of {tex}\frac{21 \sqrt{12}}{10 \sqrt{27}}{/tex}.

Solution

{tex}\frac{21 \sqrt{12}}{10 \sqrt{27}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{21 \sqrt{4 \times 3}}{10 \sqrt{9 \times 3}}{/tex}
={tex}\frac{21 \times 2 \sqrt{3}}{10 \times 3 \sqrt{3}}{/tex}

= {tex}\frac{42}{30}{/tex}
={tex}\frac{7}{5}{/tex}

Q 22[2015]

If {tex}x=\frac{5-\sqrt{3}}{5+\sqrt{3}}{/tex} and {tex}y=\frac{5+\sqrt{3}}{5-\sqrt{3}}{/tex},show that {tex}x-y=-\frac{10 \sqrt{3}}{11}{/tex}.

Solution

We know that,
{tex}x=\frac{5-\sqrt{3}}{5+\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{5-\sqrt{3}}{5+\sqrt{3}} \times \frac{5-\sqrt{3}}{5-\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(5-\sqrt{3})^{2}}{5^{2}-\sqrt{3}^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{25-10 \sqrt{3}+3}{25-3}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{28-10 \sqrt{3}}{22}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{14-5 \sqrt{3}}{11}{/tex}
Again,
{tex}y=\frac{5+\sqrt{3}}{5-\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{5+\sqrt{3}}{5-\sqrt{3}} \times \frac{5+\sqrt{3}}{5+\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(5+\sqrt{3})^{2}}{5^{2}-\sqrt{3}^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{25+10 \sqrt{3}+3}{25-3}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{28+10 \sqrt{3}}{22}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{14+5 \sqrt{3}}{11}{/tex}
x-y ={tex}\frac{14-5 \sqrt{3}}{11}-\frac{14+5 \sqrt{3}}{11}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{14-5 \sqrt{3}-14-5 \sqrt{3}}{11}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{-10 \sqrt{3}}{11}{/tex}

Q 23[2015]

Represent {tex}(1+\sqrt{9.5}){/tex} on the number line.

Solution


Steps to represent 1+{tex}\sqrt{9.5}{/tex} on number line are as followes:
1. Draw a line segment OB = 9.5 units and extend it to C such that BC = 1 unit.
2. Find the midpoint D of OC.
3. With D as centre and DO as radius, draw a semicircle.
4. Now, draw BE ⊥ AC, intersecting the semicircle at E.
Then, BE = {tex}\sqrt{9.5}{/tex} units.
5. With B as centre and BE as radius, draw an arc, meeting AC produced at F.
Then, BF = BE = {tex}\sqrt{10.5}{/tex} units.
6. Extend BF to G such that FG = 1 unit.
Then, BG = {tex}(1+\sqrt{9.5}){/tex}

Q 24[2015]

The number {tex}\frac{665}{625}{/tex} will terminate after how many decimal places?

Solution

{tex}\frac{665}{625}{/tex} =1.072

Thus, the given number will terminate after 3 decimal places. 

Q 25[2014, 2010]

Let x and y be rational and irrational numbers, respectively. Is x + y necessarily an irrational number? Give an example in support of your answer.

Solution

Here x is rational number and y is irrational number.
Yes, x + y is necessarily an irrational number.
Example: Let x = 2, which is rational.
Let y = {tex}\sqrt2 {/tex}, which is irrational.
Thus x + y = 2 + {tex}\sqrt2 {/tex} = 3.14421356...... which is irrational.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The Sum of a rational and an irrational number is always irrational.

Q 26[2014]

Rationalise the denominator and simplify: {tex}\frac{2 \sqrt{6}-\sqrt{5}}{3 \sqrt{5}-2 \sqrt{6}}{/tex}

Solution

We have,
{tex}\frac{2 \sqrt{6}-\sqrt{5}}{3 \sqrt{5}-2 \sqrt{6}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2 \sqrt{6}-\sqrt{5}}{3 \sqrt{5}-2 \sqrt{6}} \times \frac{3 \sqrt{5}+2 \sqrt{6}}{3 \sqrt{5}+2 \sqrt{6}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(2 \sqrt{6}-\sqrt{5})(3 \sqrt{5}+2 \sqrt{6})}{(3 \sqrt{5}-2 \sqrt{6})(3 \sqrt{5}+2 \sqrt{6})}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2 \sqrt{6}(3 \sqrt{5}+2 \sqrt{6})-\sqrt{5}(3 \sqrt{5}+2 \sqrt{6})}{(3 \sqrt{5})^{2}-(2 \sqrt{6})^{2}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{6 \sqrt{30}+24-15-2 \sqrt{30}}{45-24}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{4 \sqrt{30}+9}{21}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{2 \sqrt{6}-\sqrt{5}}{3 \sqrt{5}-2 \sqrt{6}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{4 \sqrt{30}+9}{21}{/tex}

Q 27[2014]

If {tex}125^{x}=\frac{25}{5^{x}}{/tex}, find the value of x.

Solution

Given,
{tex}125^{x}=\frac{25}{5^{x}} \Rightarrow\left(5^{3}\right)^{x}=\frac{5^{2}}{5^{x}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 53x = 52-x
On equating power from both sides, we get
3x = 2 - x {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x = 2
{tex}\therefore \quad x=\frac{2}{4}=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}

Q 28[2014]

Simplify : {tex}2^{\frac{2}{3}} \cdot 2^{\frac{1}{5}}{/tex}

Solution

We have.

Q 55[2011]

Find three rational numbers lying between {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{7}{8}{/tex}. How many rational numbers can be determined between these two numbers?

Solution

Since {tex}\frac{3}{5}<\frac{7}{8}{/tex}
Let {tex}x=\frac{3}{5}, y=\frac{7}{8} {/tex}
{tex}\therefore d=\frac{y-x}{n+1}=\frac{\frac{7}{8}-\frac{3}{5}}{3+1}=\frac{\frac{35-24}{40}}{4}=\frac{11}{160}{/tex}
Thus, required rational numbers between {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{7}{8}{/tex} are 
x + d, x + 2d and x + 3d
ie., {tex}\frac{3}{5}+\frac{11}{160}, \frac{3}{5}+2 \times \frac{11}{160}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{3}{5}+3 \times \frac{11}{160}{/tex}
ie., {tex}\frac{96+11}{160}, \frac{3}{5}+\frac{11}{80}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{3}{5}+\frac{33}{160}{/tex}
ie., {tex}\frac{107}{160}, \frac{48+11}{80}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{96+33}{160}{/tex}
ie., {tex}\frac{107}{160}, \frac{59}{80}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{129}{160}{/tex}

Q 61[2011]

Simplify:{tex}\left(\frac{x^{l}}{x^{m}}\right)^{\frac{1}{l m}} \cdot\left(\frac{x^{m}}{x^{n}}\right)^{\frac{1}{m n}} \cdot\left(\frac{x^{n}}{x^{l}}\right)^{\frac{1}{n l}}{/tex}

Solution

We have
{tex}\left(\frac{x^{l}}{x^{m}}\right)^{\frac{1}{l m}} \cdot\left(\frac{x^{m}}{x^{n}}\right)^{\frac{1}{m n}} \cdot\left(\frac{x^{n}}{x^{l}}\right)^{\frac{1}{n l}}{/tex}
= {tex}\left(x^{l-m}\right)^ \frac{1}{l m} \cdot\left(x^{m-n}\right)^{\frac{1}{m n}} \cdot\left(x^{n-l}\right)^{\frac{1}{n l}}{/tex}
= {tex}x^{\frac{l-m}{l m}} \cdot x^{\frac{m-n}{m n}} \cdot x^{\frac{n-l}{n l}}{/tex}
= {tex}x^{\frac{l-m}{l m}+\frac{m-n}{m n}+\frac{n-l}{n l}}{/tex}
= {tex}x^{\frac{n(l-m)+l(m-n)+m(n-l)}{l m n}}{/tex}
={tex} x^{\frac{nl-n m+l m-l n+m n-m l}{l m n}}{/tex}
= x0 = 1

Q 62[2011]

Simplify: {tex}\left(\frac{x^{p}}{x^{q}}\right)^{p+q} \cdot\left(\frac{x^{q}}{x^{r}}\right)^{q+r} \cdot\left(\frac{x^{r}}{x^{p}}\right)^{r+p}{/tex}.

Solution

{tex}\left(\frac{x^{p}}{x^{q}}\right)^{p+q} \cdot\left(\frac{x^{q}}{x^{r}}\right)^{q+r} \cdot\left(\frac{x^{r}}{x^{p}}\right)^{r+p}{/tex}
={tex}\left(x^{p-q}\right)^{p+q} \cdot\left(x^{q-r}\right)^{q+r} \cdot\left(x^{r-p}\right)^{r+p}{/tex}
= {tex}x^{(p-q)(p+q)} \cdot x^{(q-r)(q+r)} \cdot x^{(r-p)(r+p)}{/tex}
= {tex}x^{\left(p^{2}-q^{2}\right)} \cdot x^{\left(q^{2}-r^{2}\right)} \cdot x^{\left(r^{2}-p^{2}\right)}{/tex}
= {tex}x^{\left(p^{2}-q^{2}\right)+\left(q^{2}-r^{2}\right)+\left(r^{2}-p^{2}\right)}{/tex}
=x 0
= 1

{tex}=\frac{3-\sqrt{7}}{3^{2}-\sqrt{7}^{2}}+\frac{\sqrt{7}-\sqrt{5}}{\sqrt{7}^{2}-\sqrt{5}^{2}}+\frac{\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{5}^{2}-\sqrt{3}^{2}}+\frac{\sqrt{3}-1}{\sqrt{3}^{2}-1^{2}}{/tex}

{tex}=\frac{3-\sqrt{7}+\sqrt{7}-\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{5}-\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{3}-1}{2}{/tex}

= 1

It being given that {tex}\sqrt{3}={/tex} 1.732, {tex}\sqrt{5}{/tex} = 2.236, {tex}\sqrt{6}={/tex} 2.449 and {tex}\sqrt{10}={/tex} 3.162, find upto three places of decimal, {tex}\frac{3+\sqrt{5}}{3-\sqrt{5}}{/tex}.

Solution

{tex}\frac{3+\sqrt{5}}{3-\sqrt{5}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{3+\sqrt{5}}{3-\sqrt{5}} \times \frac{3+\sqrt{5}}{3+\sqrt{5}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{(3+\sqrt{5})^{2}}{3^{3}-\sqrt{5}^{2}}{/tex} [ a2 - b2 = (a + b)(a - b)]
{tex}=\frac{3^{2}+2 \times 3 \sqrt{5}+\sqrt{5}^{2}}{9-5}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{9+6 \sqrt{5}+5}{4}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{14+6 \sqrt{5}}{4}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{7+3 \sqrt{5}}{2}{/tex}
Substituting the value {tex}\sqrt{5}{/tex} we get,
{tex}\frac{7+3 \times 2.236}{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{7+6.708}{2}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{13.708}{2}{/tex}
= 6.854

Q 90[2010]

If {tex}p=\frac{3-\sqrt{5}}{3+\sqrt{5}}{/tex} and {tex}q=\frac{3+\sqrt{5}}{3-\sqrt{5}}{/tex}, find the value of p2 + q2.

 p2 + q2

Q 92[2010]

Write the following in the descending order of magnitude. {tex}\sqrt[3]{2}, \sqrt{3}, \sqrt[6]{5}{/tex}.

Solution

Given
{tex}\sqrt[3]{2}=2^{\frac{1}{3}} ; \sqrt{3}=3^{\frac{1}{2}} ; \sqrt[6]{5}=5^{\frac{1}{6}}{/tex}
LCM of 3,2 and 6 = 6
{tex}\therefore \quad 2^{\frac{1}{3}}=2^{\left(\frac{1}{3} \times \frac{2}{2}\right)}=2^{\frac{2}{6}}=\left(2^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{6}}=4^{\frac{1}{6}}{/tex}
{tex}3^{\frac{1}{2}}=3^{\left(\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{3}{3}\right)}=3^{\frac{3}{6}}=\left(3^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{6}}=(27)^{\frac{1}{6}}{/tex}
Clearly, {tex}(27)^{\frac{1}{6}}>5^{\frac{1}{6}}>4^{\frac{1}{6}}{/tex}
So, {tex}3^{\frac{1}{2}}>5^{\frac{1}{6}}>2^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex} 
or {tex}\sqrt{3}>\sqrt[6]{5}>\sqrt[3]{2}{/tex}
Hence, the correct descending order is {tex}\sqrt{3}, \sqrt[6]{5}{/tex} and {tex}\sqrt[3]{2}{/tex}.

Q 93[2010]

Evaluate: {tex}\left(\frac{81}{16}\right)^{-\frac{3}{4}}\left(\left(\frac{25}{9}\right)^{-\frac{3}{2}} \div\left(\frac{5}{2}\right)^{-3}\right){/tex}.

Solution

{tex}\left(\frac{81}{16}\right)^{-\frac{3}{4}}\left[\left(\frac{25}{9}\right)^{-\frac{3}{2}} \div\left(\frac{5}{2}\right)^{-3}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{16^{\frac{3}{4}}}{81}\left[\left(\frac{9}{25}\right)^{\frac{3}{2}} \div\left(\frac{2}{5}\right)^{3}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\left(\frac{2^{4}}{3^{4}}\right)^{\frac{3}{4}}\left[\left(\frac{3^{2}}{5^{2}}\right)^{\frac{3}{2}} \div \frac{2^{3}}{5^{3}}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2^{4 \times \frac{3}{4}}}{3^{4 \times \frac{3}{4}}}\left[\frac{3^{2 \times \frac{3}{2}}}{5^{2 \times \frac{3}{2}}} \div \frac{8}{125}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2^{3}}{3^{3}}\left[\frac{3^{3}}{5^{3}} \times \frac{125}{8}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{8}{27}\left[\frac{27}{125} \times \frac{125}{8}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{8}{27} \times \frac{27}{8}{/tex}
= 1

Q 97[2010]

If x = 3 + {tex}2 \sqrt{2}{/tex} check whether {tex}x+\frac{1}{x}{/tex} is rational or irrational.

Solution

Given
{tex}x=3+2 \sqrt{2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex}= {tex}\frac{1}{3+2\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
x +{tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex} = {tex}(3+2 \sqrt{2})+\frac{1}{3+2 \sqrt{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=3+2 \sqrt{2}+\frac{1}{3+2 \sqrt{2}} \times \frac{3-2 \sqrt{2}}{3-2 \sqrt{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=3+2 \sqrt{2}+\frac{3-2 \sqrt{2}}{3^{2}-2 \sqrt{2}^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=3+2 \sqrt{2}+\frac{3-2 \sqrt{2}}{9-8}{/tex}
{tex}=3+2 \sqrt{2}+\frac{3-2 \sqrt{2}}{1}{/tex}
{tex}=3+2 \sqrt{2}+3-2 \sqrt{2}{/tex}
= 6
Therefore, {tex}x+\frac{1}{x}{/tex} is rational.

Q 98[2010]

If {tex}a=3-2 \sqrt{2}{/tex}, find the value of {tex}a^{2}-\frac{1}{a^{2}}{/tex}.

Solution

Given
a= {tex}3-2 \sqrt{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2{tex}=(3-2 \sqrt{2})^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=3^{2}-2 \times 3 \times 2 \sqrt{2}+(2 \sqrt{2})^{2}{/tex}
{tex}=9-12 \sqrt{2}+8{/tex}
{tex}=17-12 \sqrt{2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1}{x^2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{17-12\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
={tex}\frac{1}{17-12 \sqrt{2}} \times \frac{17+12 \sqrt{2}}{17+12 \sqrt{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{17+12 \sqrt{2}}{17^{2}-(12 \sqrt{2})^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{17+12 \sqrt{2}}{289-288}{/tex}
{tex}=17+12 \sqrt{2}{/tex}
So {tex}a^{2}-\frac{1}{a^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}(17-12 \sqrt{2})-(17+12 \sqrt{2}){/tex}
{tex}=17-12 \sqrt{2}-17-12 \sqrt{2}{/tex}
{tex}=-24 \sqrt{2}{/tex}

Q 1[2020]

The total surface area of a solid hemisphere is:

a

{tex}2 \pi r^{2}{/tex}

✓

{tex}3 \pi r^{2}{/tex}

c

{tex}4 \pi r^{2}{/tex}

d

{tex}\frac{2}{3} \pi r^{2}{/tex}

Solution

Curved surface area of a solid hemisphere will be half the surface area of a solid sphere.
Hence, curved surface area of solid hemisphere =  {tex} \frac12 \times{/tex}4{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 And, the flat surface area of hemisphere will be the area of circle with radius r. Hence, the flat surface area of hemisphere = {tex}\pi{/tex}r2
Total surface area of a solid hemisphere with radius r is, S = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 +{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = 3{tex}\pi{/tex}r2

Q 2[2017]

Volume and surface area of a solid hemisphere are numerically equal. What is the diameter of the hemisphere?

Solution

Let the radius of the hemisphere be r units.
The volume of a hemisphere = Surface area of the hemisphere
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{2}{3} \pi r^{3}{/tex} = {tex}3 \pi r^{2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{2}{3}r{/tex} = 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = {tex}\frac 92{/tex}
=> d =2r = 9 units
Hence, the diameter of the hemisphere is equal to 9 units.
